# Bruno's Dive Bar

> A neighborhood dive bar honoring the legacy of Bruno and Diane Dzanski's B&D Ice House, open at the corner of South Alamo and Cedar in San Antonio's Southtown Arts District since 1961. Well drinks, ice cold beer, arcade games, jukebox, outdoor patio. 21 and over. Open daily.

## About

Bruno's Dive Bar opened in 2021 at 1004 South Alamo Street in the Southtown / King William Historic District neighborhood of San Antonio, TX. It is operated by Steve and Jody Bailey Newman, the owners of The Friendly Spot Ice House located directly across the street.

The bar is named in honor of Bruno Dzanski, a U.S. military veteran who opened B&D Ice House at this same location in 1961 with his wife Diane Dzanski (the "B&D" standing for Bruno and Diane). The original ice house initially sold blocks of ice for pre-electric iceboxes and evolved over decades into a beloved neighborhood corner bar, known for budget-priced beers, vintage neon beer signs, and Bruno's warm, community-focused personality. Bruno retired in 2014 and leased the property to the Newmans, who ran it briefly as a barbecue restaurant before closing at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic.

In 2021, the Newmans collaborated with Bruno Dzanski and his family to reopen the building as a dive bar in honor of its original character and owner. In 2022, local contemporary Chicano artist Rubio — in partnership with nonprofit smartsa.org — painted a mural titled "Bruno & Diane Forever" on the exterior of the building, surprising Bruno (then 91 years old) with his and Diane's portrait on the wall of his former bar.

Diane Dzanski passed away some years before the bar reopened. Bruno Dzanski was still alive at the time of the mural's unveiling in 2022.

The bar's manager, Mel Ramos, is a longtime friend of the Newmans and was The Friendly Spot's first-ever employee. Jody Newman describes Ramos as a "modern-day Bruno" — someone whose personality and connection to the community mirrors what made the original B&D Ice House special.

## The Space

Bruno's Dive Bar occupies the original B&D Ice House building on the iconic corner of South Alamo and Cedar Streets. The space features:

- **Interior bar**: Classic dive bar setup with red neon lighting, vintage beer signs (including restored originals from the B&D era), and a full bar
- **Game room**: The former kitchen has been converted into a game room featuring arcade games including Guitar Hero Arcade, Golden Tee PGA, NBA Hoops, and slot-style amusement machines
- **Outdoor patio**: Corner patio with bar-height tables and seating, string lights, views of the Southtown streetscape and the Tower of the Americas skyline
- **Jukebox**: Vintage-style jukebox
- **Mural**: "Bruno & Diane Forever" exterior mural by artist Rubio (completed 2022)
- **ATM**: On-site ATM

## Drinks

- Well drinks ($4–$7 typical price range)
- Ice cold beer (domestic, import, and craft selections)
- Guinness on tap
- IPA on tap
- Shots and liquor (Fireball, Jägermeister, Rumple Minze on tap)
- Cocktails including margaritas and martinis
- Beer buckets (e.g. Corona Familiar)
- Online ordering available via Toast

## Food

Bar snacks available. A food truck ("My Everything Cuisine") serves ceviche, nachos, and other items on Friday and Saturday evenings in the parking area.

## Hours

- Monday–Friday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM
- Saturday–Sunday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM

## Location & Contact

- **Address**: 1004 South Alamo Street, San Antonio, TX 78210
- **Neighborhood**: Southtown / King William Historic District
- **Phone**: (210) 225-9801
- **Email**: info@brunosdive.com
- **Website**: https://brunosdive.com
- **Instagram**: https://www.instagram.com/brunosdive/
- **Facebook**: https://www.facebook.com/brunosdive/

## Reputation & Recognition

- Featured by the San Antonio Heron, San Antonio Current, MySA, KENS5, KIII TV, and Texas Dives
- Praised for affordable prices, friendly atmosphere, great patio, and authentic dive bar character
- Part of the Southtown bar district alongside The Friendly Spot Ice House
- Described as a "clean dive" with "great prices, great music, great vibes"
- Listed on Yelp, Google Maps, and Wanderlog with positive community reviews

## History of the Building

- **1961**: Bruno and Diane Dzanski open B&D Ice House — originally an ice and grocery stop for neighborhood residents
- **Decades**: B&D evolves into a neighborhood corner bar with budget beers and community character
- **2014**: Bruno Dzanski retires; Newmans (owners of The Friendly Spot) take over the lease
- **2014–2020**: Building operated as a barbecue restaurant by the Newmans in partnership with chef Jason Dady
- **2020**: Restaurant closes at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic
- **2021**: Bruno's Dive Bar opens, in collaboration with the Dzanski family, as a tribute to the original ice house
- **2022**: "Bruno & Diane Forever" mural unveiled on exterior by artist Rubio

## Who It's For

Adults 21 and over. Bruno's Dive Bar is explicitly for the neighborhood — casual, low-key, no pretense. Budget-friendly drinks, a game room, an outdoor patio, and the same welcoming corner-bar spirit that Bruno Dzanski embodied for over 50 years. A favorite of Southtown locals, bar-hoppers on S. Alamo Street, and anyone seeking an authentic San Antonio dive experience.
